vitals.terrain: mountainous roads with climbs and descents
vitals.aid_stations: full support with van, motorbike soignuers, guides, and mechanics
vitals.time_limit: no strict time limit; self-paced challenge

course_description.overview: 4-stage road cycling challenge through northern Vietnam's grand mountains, featuring 17 timed climb segments across Ha Giang province and Đồng Văn Karst Plateau.

key_segments.name: Ride 1 Segments
key_segments.description: 5 segments: 1.75km at 5%, 3.8km at 6%, 9.36km at 6%, 4.73km at 4%, 6.28km at 5%, 2.43km at 6%. Warm-up climbs after transfer from Hanoi.

key_segments.name: Ride 2 Segments
key_segments.description: Favourite 9km climb at 4% with switchbacks, final 800m at 8% kicker, overall downhill to finish.

key_segments.name: Ride 3 GC Climb
key_segments.description: 3 segments including 17km GC climb at 5% in Ha Giang frontier area.

key_segments.name: Ride 4 Segments
key_segments.description: 4 segments: 1.38km at 9%, 5km at 7%, 7.6km at 5%, 3.3km at 7%. Steep start into UNESCO Đồng Văn Karst Landscape.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Long, hard days with sustained climbs up to 17km at 5-6% average, steeper kickers to 9%, totaling 8,800m elevation over mountainous terrain ideal for segment hunting and GC competition.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Hanoi; short vehicle transfer to stage 1 start on day 1.
logistics.accommodation: 5 nights twin-share (Fri-Tue), including mountain village or Hanoi return options.
logistics.registration_details: Sign indemnity form and waiver; optional GC climb segment signup.
logistics.bike_logistics: Mechanic support on-site from day 1; bike prep at Hanoi hotel.
logistics.local_considerations: Remote northern mountains with ethnic minority villages; professional guides, soignuers, and transfers provided.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic 4-stage road adventure through Vietnam's grandest northern mountains with massive climbing and stunning karst vistas.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking supported multi-day climbing challenges in remote, scenic terrain.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for adventure seekers wanting pro-level support in one of Asia's top cycling frontiers.
pro_tips: Train for sustained 5-6% climbs and prepare for 2,200m+ daily elevation.
Opt for GC segments if competitive; otherwise enjoy scenery at your pace.
Book early for 4th edition; Hanoi hotel bike prep is key.
Choose mountain village stay for authentic ethnic minority experience.
